name: CALCIUM PYROPHOSPHATE DIHYDRATE DEPOSITION DISEASE
definition: CPPD = PSEUDOGOUT = FAMILIAL CHONDROCALCINOSIS metabolic arthropathy characterized by deposition of calcium pyrophosphate dihydrate

demographics.Prevalence: widespread in older population; increasing with age; M÷F = 3÷2

discriminating_features: polyarticular chondrocalcinosis (in fibro- and hyaline cartilage)
numerous intraarticular bodies (fragmentation of subchondral bone)
coalescing CPPD crystals → tophus-like deposits (rare) → significant articular destruction
joint space narrowing + extensive subchondral sclerosis

general: polyarticular chondrocalcinosis (in fibro- and hyaline cartilage)
numerous intraarticular bodies (fragmentation of subchondral bone)
coalescing CPPD crystals → tophus-like deposits (rare) → significant articular destruction
joint space narrowing + extensive subchondral sclerosis
Hand: resembling degenerative joint disease (without DIP and PIP involvement)
small hook-like osteophytes at radial aspect of metacarpal heads 2 & 3
Wrist: extensive narrowing / obliteration of joint space between distal radius + scaphoid →
incorporation of scaphoid into articular surface of radius
scapholunate separation (= ligament tear) → scapholunate advanced collapse (SLAC
Knee: medial femorotibial + patellofemoral compartments commonly involved simultaneously
Spine: chondrocalcinosis / calcifications of outer fibers of annulus fibrosus of lumbar spine
vertical radiodense line in symphysis pubis
